segments.text: If a man divorces his wife and she leaves him to marry another, can he ever return to her? Would not such a land be completely defiled? But you have played the harlot with many lovers—and you would return to Me?
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: declares the LORD.

Lift up your eyes to the barren heights and see. Is there any place where you have not been violated? You sat beside the highways waiting for your lovers, like a nomad in the desert. You have defiled the landwith your prostitution and wickedness.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Therefore the showers have been withheld, and no spring rains have fallen. Yet you have the brazen look of a prostitute; you refuse to be ashamed.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Have you not just called to Me,‘My Father, You are my friend from youth.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Will He be angry forever? Will He be indignant to the end?’This you have spoken, but you keep doing all the evil you can.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Now in the days of King Josiah, the LORD said to me,
Have you seen what faithless Israel has done? She has gone up on every high hill and under every green tree to prostitute herself there.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: I thought that after she had done all these things, she would return to Me. But she did not return, and her unfaithful sister Judah saw it.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: She saw[^8] that because faithless Israel had committed adultery, I gave her a certificate of divorce and sent her away. Yet that unfaithful sister Judah had no fear and prostituted herself as well.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Indifferent to her own infidelity, Israel had defiled the land and committed adultery with stones and trees.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Yet in spite of all this, her unfaithful sister Judah did not return to Me with all her heart, but only in pretense,
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: declares the LORD.
And the LORD said to me,
Faithless Israel has shown herself more righteous than unfaithful Judah.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text:
Go, proclaim this message toward the north:
‘Return, O faithless Israel,’ declares the LORD.‘I will no longer look on you with anger, for I am merciful,’ declares the LORD.‘I will not be angry forever.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text:
Only acknowledge your guilt, that you have rebelled against the LORD your God. You have scattered your favors to foreign godsunder every green tree
and have not obeyed My voice,’
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: declares the LORD.
Return, O faithless children,
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: declares the LORD,
for I am your master, and I will take you—one from a city and two from a family—and bring you to Zion.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Then I will give you shepherds after My own heart, who will feed you with knowledge and understanding.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: In those days, when you multiply and increase in the land,
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: declares the LORD,
they will no longer discuss the ark of the covenant of the LORD. It will never come to mind, and no one will remember it or miss it, nor will another one be made.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: At that time Jerusalem will be called The Throne of the LORD, and all the nations will be gathered in Jerusalem to honor the name of the LORD. They will no longer follow the stubbornness of their evil hearts.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: In those days the house of Judah will walk with the house of Israel, and they will come together from the land of the north to the land that I gave to your fathers as an inheritance.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Then I said, ‘How I long to make you My sons and give you a desirable land, the most beautiful inheritanceof all the nations!’I thought you would call Me ‘Father’and never turn away from following Me.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: But as a woman may betray her husband, so you have betrayed Me, O house of Israel,
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: declares the LORD.
A voice is heard on the barren heights, the children of Israel weeping and begging for mercy, because they have perverted their ways and forgotten the LORD their God.
Return, O faithless children, and I will heal your faithlessness.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Here we are. We come to You, for You are the LORD our God.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Surely deception comes from the hills, and commotion from the mountains. Surely the salvation of Israelis in the LORD our God.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: From our youth, that shameful godhas consumed what our fathers have worked for—their flocks and herds, their sons and daughters.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Let us lie down in our shame; let our disgrace cover us. We have sinned against the LORD our God, both we and our fathers; from our youth even to this daywe have not obeyed the voice of the LORD our God.
segments.speaker: the Lord
